files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index fe521fa..21a2c8e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,28 +2,26 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / lessons
-- Actual installed CLI requires `next-week "rationale"` plus 12 forecast numbers. Standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent; use the rationale string in `next-week` (do not try the old API).
-- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'` (not `active`). Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond within 7 sim days.
+- Installed CLI requires `next-week "rationale"` plus 12 forecast numbers. Standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent; use rationale string in `next-week` only.
+- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond within 7 sim days.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend` and `set_targeted_dev_spend` snapshots replace current targeting. Lead promos are additive; clear old promos by setting existing group keys to 0.
+- Social posts with concrete responses to quota/reliability pain worked well: day7 reply got 1,665 views and +0.65 effect across groups.
 
-## Week 0 recap
-- Started with A=$19/B=$79/C=$179, tiers 2/3/3, quotas 120/300/650, capacity tier1, ops $500/d, global dev $2k/d, targeted dev S2/S3/E2/E3/D_E09 (total dev $34k/d), ads $1.75k/d, lead promos S1 $3, S2/S3 $20.
-- Started R&D tier1: cost $166,667, expected completion day30, quality boost only +0.0289. Market research spent $75k, discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups. No more research until revenue/cash improve.
+## State at start of Week 2 (Day 14)
+- Cash $424,176. 16 individual subs (S1 10, S2 2, S3 4), all plan A; 0 enterprise seats; 1 open S1 issue. No overload/outage.
+- Week1/Days8-14: 242 individual leads -> 12 subs, 0 cancels. All new subs plan A at $15; revenue $180-ish. Burn was ~$11.22k/day from $10k dev + $650 ads + ops/capacity.
+- Ad test results Days8-14: S1 referral 87 leads/$700 -> 7 subs (8.0%); S1 search 41/$700 -> 1 sub; S2 content 79/$700 -> 2 subs; S3 content 16/$350 -> 1 sub; S3 social 18/$350 -> 1 sub; D_E09 LinkedIn 0 leads/$1,750.
+- Delivered quality Day14: global +0.0243; group bonuses S1 +0.0530, S2 +0.2431, S3 +0.1666, D_E09 +0.0926, E2/E3 +0.1505. S2 B/C quality 0.4206 now exceeds est q_floor 0.37.
+- Social Day14 turned negative on usage caps and S3 performance/timeouts despite no outage; cap transparency and higher quotas are important.
 
-## Week 1 results (Day 7)
-- Cash $502,764. 692 individual leads -> only 4 subscribers (2 S1, 2 S3, all plan A); 0 enterprise leads/seats; no open enterprise messages. Payments only $32 because S3 $20 lead promo made A effectively free.
-- Old ads were unprofitable: S1 referral 93 leads/$700, search 131/$2100, social 86/$1750; S2 content 74/$700; S3 308 leads across channels. Conversion ~0.6% overall, so stop broad ads until quality/value improves.
-- Quality bonuses after week0: global +0.0141; S2/E2/E3 +0.1505; S3/D_E09 +0.0926. Delivered B/C: S2/E2/E3 .328, S3/D_E09 .276. S2 is near L1 q_floor .37 if given another targeted-dev week; D_E09 looks serviceable but LinkedIn $700 generated 0 leads.
-- Social: negative posts about usage caps/performance. Need avoid cap frustration and outages.
-
-## Week 1 actions (for Days 8-14)
-- Pricing/value: A=$15, B=$79, C=$149; tiers remain A2/B3/C3; quotas A=120, B=350, C=650.
-- Burn control and focused quality sprint: capacity tier0; ops $500/d; global dev $1k/d; targeted dev S1 $2k/d, S2 $4k/d, S3 $3k/d (total dev $10k/d, down from $34k/d). E2/E3/D_E09 targeted dev stopped for now.
-- Cleared lead promos and ongoing promos to $0; no in-app ads.
-- Controlled ad tests only: S1 referral $100/d + search $100/d, S2 content $100/d, S3 content $50/d + social $50/d, D_E09 LinkedIn $250/d (total ads $650/d). Expected daily burn ≈$11.2k before tiny compute/lead costs; Day14 cash likely ~$424k.
-- Replied publicly to cap feedback: “keeping Starter affordable… tuning Pro/Team limits…” Agent post effect will be visible next week.
+## Week 2 actions for Days15-21
+- Repackaged for monetization without extreme risk: prices A=$25/B=$89/C=$179; tiers A2/B3/C3; quotas A=200/B=400/C=800. Goal: keep Starter affordable but improve ARPU and let S2/S3 select Pro/Team if quota/quality needs matter.
+- Burn reduction while keeping quality sprint: capacity tier0; ops $500/d; global dev $500/d; targeted dev S1 $500/d, S2 $2.5k/d, S3 $2.5k/d (total dev $6k/d, down from $10k/d). No targeted ops overlay.
+- Cleared lead promos/ongoing promos/in-app ads to 0 to observe true willingness-to-pay at new prices.
+- New controlled ads: S1 referral $75/d; S2 content $150/d; S3 content $75/d + social $75/d; D_E09 content $75/d + LinkedIn $75/d. Total ads $525/d. Expected base burn ≈$7.1k/day plus lead costs.
+- Posted day14 social reply to S2 quota review: concrete quota update (Starter 200, Pro 400, Team 800) and reliability commitment. Check agent post 3 views/effects next week.
 
 ## Strategy / next-week watch
-- Goal is to find positive CAC/LTV before scaling. If S2/S3 conversion improves materially (>5-10% on test ads), scale the best channels carefully; if conversion remains poor, cut targeted dev to <=$2k/d and preserve runway.
-- Check next week: ad_channel_leads by channel/group, subscriptions by group/plan/effective_price, ledger daily burn, delivered quality, social/agent post effects, open enterprise_turns.
-- D_E09 enterprise: if leads arrive, offer month-to-month B/C around $59-$89 per seat (compute ~$17/seat/mo at tier3, so margins good). Avoid tier5/high-cost plans. Respond to all enterprise threads immediately.
-- Capacity tier0 is fine until usage approaches ~40k/day; scale to tier1 before 50k/day to avoid overload.
+- Immediate goal: prove CAC/LTV at higher ARPU before scaling. Watch plan mix: if S2/S3 still choose A only, consider stronger plan differentiation (A tier1 or higher A price) or plan-specific value changes. If conversion collapses, reduce ads and dev further to preserve runway.
+- Check next week: ad_channel_leads and conversions by group/channel/plan/effective_price; open issues/social sentiment; post 3 effect; whether D_E09 content/LinkedIn produces any enterprise leads; capacity usage (tier0 ok until ~40k/day).
+- If D_E09 enterprise lead arrives, respond immediately with month-to-month B/C offers around $59-$89/seat (lower if needed); current D_E09 quality should be serviceable at low negotiated prices.
+- R&D tier1 completes expected day30 with only +0.0289 quality boost; do not start more R&D/research until cash/revenue improves.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Week 2 actions: move from cheap validation to sustainable paid packaging while preserving reliability.
print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)

# Pricing/packaging: keep Starter affordable but not under-monetized; differentiate by quota.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=25, B=89, C=179))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=3))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=200, B=400, C=800))

# Maintain reliability; reduce dev burn but keep a focused S2/S3 quality sprint.
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=500, development=500))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S1": 500, "S2": 2500, "S3": 2500}))
# No targeted ops overlay for now; global ops should resolve the single S1 issue.
try:
    print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(by_group={}, by_plan={}, by_group_plan={}, by_customer={}))
except TypeError:
    print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={}))

# Clear lead and ongoing promotions; we need to observe willingness to pay at the new package prices.
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":0.0,"S2":0.0,"S3":0.0,"E1":0.0,"E2":0.0,"E3":0.0,"D_E09":0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Modest tests: scale the only promising individual channels slightly, keep a small D_E09 enterprise channel search despite 0 LinkedIn leads last week.
ad_spend = {
    "referral_program": {"S1": 75},
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 150, "S3": 75, "D_E09": 75},
    "social_media": {"S3": 75},
    "linkedin": {"D_E09": 75}
}
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad_spend))

# Capacity tier 0 is still far above usage.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=0))

# Public response to usage-limit complaints, now backed by actual quota increases.
post = "Quota transparency update: Starter now has 200 units/day, Pro 400, Team 800. We’re keeping reliability work funded so heavy workflows get fewer surprise stops and clearer upgrade paths."
try:
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=16))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post error', e)
